Danny: My name is Danny Perasa, and this is my wife Annie. We've been married for over sixty years.

Annie: Sixty-three years.

Danny: Sixty-three years. And every single day, I leave Annie a love note. I write it on a piece of paper, and I put it somewhere she'll find it. Sometimes in her purse, sometimes on the kitchen table, sometimes on her pillow.

Annie: He's been doing this for years. Every single day. I never know where I'll find it, but I always do.

Danny: I write simple things. "I love you." "You make me happy." "You are beautiful." Sometimes I write longer notes about our life together, about our memories.

Annie: When I find the note, I read it, and I smile. It makes my day. It reminds me that after all these years, he still loves me. He still cares.

Danny: I do it because I want her to know. I want her to know every day that she is loved, that she is important to me. Life is short, and I don't want to waste a single day without telling her how I feel.

Annie: One day, I asked him, "Danny, when you leave me, how am I going to live? How am I going to live without your notes?"

Danny: I told her, "Annie, I've written enough notes. I've written enough for the rest of your life. You'll find them. I've hidden them everywhere. In books, in drawers, in your favorite places. You'll find them when you need them most."

Annie: He's right. I've found notes in the strangest places. In old books, in my jewelry box, even in my coat pocket from years ago. He's been preparing for this. He's been writing notes for my future, for when he's gone.

Danny: I love her. I've loved her for sixty-three years, and I'll love her forever. These notes are my way of being with her, even when I can't be there.

Annie: We've had a beautiful life together. We've raised children, we've traveled, we've laughed, we've cried. But through it all, there's been one constant: his love notes. They remind me that love doesn't end. It continues, even after someone is gone.

Danny: That's what I want her to remember. That's what I want her to know. That love is forever. That we'll always be a family, no matter what happens.

Annie: We'll always be a family. Always.
